care.summary: Bold tropical with large heart-shaped leaves; edible corms used in many cuisines.
care.water: High; thrives in boggy or aquatic conditions
care.soil: Rich, moisture-retentive soil; can grow in water
care.toxicity: Corms must be cooked before eating (raw is toxic)
common_issues: Fungal spots in still, humid air
tips: Great for pond edges or rain gardens
Die back in winter; store corms in cool dry place
Use as a dramatic focal point in shade gardens
